diffCoverage.notes: All eight changed source files reviewed. Sticker DTOs carry only non-secret display data (ids + render URL). zalo/sticker_image_url points to the already-allowlisted CSP host. SQL is parameterized throughout. No credential or token crosses IPC.

findings.evidence: Sticker DTO is pure display data: sticker id/catId/type (integers Zalo uses as opaque ids) plus a render URL on the allowlisted CSP host. No credential, cookie, or token field. serde rename_all=camelCase matches frontend conventions.

sticker_image_url builds the CDN URL from a numeric id only, pointing to zalo-api.zadn.vn (already in the CSP img-src allowlist). No user-controlled path component. sticker_from_content reads numeric ids from a JSON value (defensive: as_i64 also tolerates stringized ids); id=0 is rejected.

All sticker SQL is parameterized: save_sticker_message binds sticker.id/cat_id/sticker_type/url as typed parameters, record_recent_sticker binds account_id + sticker fields, load_recent_stickers binds account_id + limit. No string interpolation or dynamic SQL construction.

send_sticker validates thread_id is non-empty and sticker.id != 0 before delegating. search_stickers validates keyword is non-empty and clamps limit to 1-200. recent_stickers/sticker_categories read from the local store only (no network). All commands return non-secret DTOs.
Sticker render URL host (zalo-api.zadn.vn) is already in the CSP img-src directive (https://zalo-api.zadn.vn). No CSP change needed.

summary: Security review: sticker payloads carry only non-secret display data (numeric ids + render URL). No credential or token crosses IPC. Sticker render URL host matches the existing CSP allowlist. SQL is parameterized throughout. Input validation at the command boundary (non-empty keyword, non-empty thread_id, sticker.id != 0). The sticker body '[Sticker]' stored in plaintext SQLite matches the already-accepted message-cache posture (public display data, not a bearer token). Approve.
